zh.Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可波斯頓洛波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joshua Child於大約1658年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利時，他的父親Benjamin Child28歲，母親Mary Bowen25歲。他於1685年3月9日與Elizabeth Morris在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1730年1月18日在他的家鄉享年73歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州素福爾可波斯頓洛斯林達爾沃爾特 史崔特 波英 格洛德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joshua Child于大约1658年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利时，他的父亲Benjamin Child28岁，母亲Mary Bowen25岁。他于1685年3月9日与Elizabeth Morris在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1730年1月18日在他的家乡享年73岁，埋葬在美国马萨诸塞州素福爾可波斯頓洛斯林達爾沃爾特 史崔特 波英 格洛德。

nameMeanings.name: Child
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Middle English child ‘child, infant’ (Old English cild), in various possible applications. The word is found in Old English as a byname, and in Middle English as a widely used affectionate term of address. It was also used as a term of status for a young man of noble birth, although the exact meaning is not clear; in the 13th and 14th centuries it was a technical term used of a young noble awaiting elevation to the knighthood. In other cases it may have been applied as a byname to a youth considerably younger than his brothers or to one who was a minor on the death of his father.
in Kent, possibly a topographic name from Old English cielde ‘spring (water)’, a rare word derived from c(e)ald ‘cold’.

variants.name: Barnes
meanings: English: habitational name from Barnes (on the Surrey bank of the Thames in London), named with Old English bere-ærn ‘barn, a storehouse for barley and other grain’, or a topographic name or metonymic occupational name for someone who lived by or worked at a barn or barns, from Middle English barn ‘barn, granary’.

English: variant of Barne, with excrescent -s, derived from either the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn (based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn or Old English Beorn, both from a word meaning ‘warrior’), or from Middle English barn (Old Norse barn) ‘child’. The latter term is found as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child (see Child ).
Irish: in Ireland in many cases this is no doubt the English name, but in others it is possibly an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Bearáin ‘descendant of Bearán’, a byname meaning ‘spear’.

variants.name: Chill
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Derbyshire): variant of   Child   or   Chell  .

variants.name: Childs
meanings: English: variant of Child , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Chilton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Chilton, for example in Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, County Durham, Hampshire, Kent, Shropshire, Somerset, Suffolk, and Wiltshire. The majority are shown by early forms to derive from Old English cild ‘child’ (see Child ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. One place of this name in Somerset possibly gets its first element from Old English cealc ‘chalk, limestone’, and one on the Isle of Wight from the personal name Cēola (compare Chilcott ), or from Old English ceole ‘deep valley’.

variants.name: Bambino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Angelo.

Italian: from Bambino, a byname meaning ‘child’ (see Child ), or a personal name given with reference to Gesù Bambino ‘the Christ child’, the use of Gesù as a personal name being considered taboo in Italy.

variants.name: Chilson
meanings: English: habitational name from Chilson in Oxfordshire, named with Old English cild ‘young man’ (see Child ) + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’.

History: It is not known when this surname was first brought to America, but it was already established in CT in the early 18th century. Daniel Chilson of Weathersfield, CT, was born c. 1720 and on 4 October 1745 married Sybil Stanclift in Middlesex County, CT.

variants.name: Fairchild
meanings: English (southern): nickname from Middle English fair child ‘handsome child’ or ‘handsome youth of noble birth’ (see Child ).

variants.name: Childers
meanings: English: probably a habitational name from some lost place called Childerhouse, from Old English cildra, genitive plural of cild ‘child’ + hūs ‘house’. This may have referred to some form of orphanage perhaps run by a religious order, or perhaps the first element is to be understood in its later sense as a term of status (see Child ).

nameMeanings.name: Joshua
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Meaning ‘God is salvation’ in Hebrew; it is borne in the Bible by the Israelite leader who took command of the Children of Israel after the death of Moses and led them, after many battles, to take possession of the Promised Land. The name, long favoured by Jews and Nonconformist Christians, enjoyed a great surge in popularity in the 1990s. Well-known bearers of the name include the American pianist and conductor  Joshua Rifkin  (  b. 1944 ) and the American-born violinist  Joshua Bell  (  b. 1967 ).

historicEvents.description: Oldest grave seen in memorial list.

en.lifeSummary: When Joshua Child was born about 1658, in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, his father, Benjamin Child, was 28 and his mother, Mary Bowen, was 25. He married Elizabeth Morris on 9 March 1685, in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 18 January 1730, in his hometown, at the age of 73, and was buried in Walter Street Burying Ground, Roslindale, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joshua Child est né environ 1658, à Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Benjamin Child, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Mary Bowen, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Morris le 9 mars 1685, à Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 18 janvier 1730, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterré à Walter Street Burying Ground, Roslindale, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joshua Child um 1658 in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater Benjamin Child 28 und seine Mutter Mary Bowen 25. Er hat am 9. März 1685 in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Elizabeth Morris geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 18. Januar 1730 im Alter von 73 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Walter Street Burying Ground, Roslindale, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joshua Child è nato il circa 1658 nel Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Benjamin Child, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Mary Bowen, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Morris il 9 marzo 1685, nel Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 18 gennaio 1730, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 73 anni, e fu sepolto a Walter Street Burying Ground, Roslindale, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colo: イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリー
ja.Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts: イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクボストンロクスブリー
ja.lifeSummary: Joshua Childが約1658年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーで生まれた時，父Benjamin Childは28歳，母Mary Bowenは25歳であった。1685年3月9日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーでElizabeth Morrisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1730年1月18日に故郷で73歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州スフォルクボストンロスリンダルワルター ストレエト ブリイング グロウンドにて埋葬された。

ko.Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴 록스버이
ko.of Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay, B: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴
ko.Walter Street Burying Ground, Roslindale: 미국 매사추세츠 주 수폴크 보스턴 로슬린데일 월터 스트릿 버이잉 그라운드
ko.Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colo: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이
ko.Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 보스턴 록스버이
ko.lifeSummary: Joshua Child(이)가 대략 1658년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Benjamin Child(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Bowen(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1685년 3월 9일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에서 Elizabeth Morris(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1730년 1월 18일 그의 고향에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 수폴크 보스턴 로슬린데일 월터 스트릿 버이잉 그라운드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joshua Child nasceu aproximadamente 1658, em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, Benjamin Child, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Mary Bowen, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Morris em 9 de março de 1685, em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 18 de janeiro de 1730, em sua cidade natal, com 73 anos, e foi sepultado em Walter Street Burying Ground, Roslindale, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.Walter Street Burying Ground, Roslindale: валтир стрит буринг гроунд, рослиндеил, бостон, суффолк, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты
ru.Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colo: роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts: роксбури, бостон, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joshua Child родился около 1658 г. в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Benjamin Child было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Bowen, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Morris 9 марта 1685 г. в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он умер 18 января 1730 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 73 лет и был похоронен в местности валтир стрит буринг гроунд, рослиндеил, бостон, суффолк, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joshua Child nació aproximadamente 1658, en Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, Benjamin Child, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Mary Bowen, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Morris el 9 de marzo de 1685, en Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 18 de enero de 1730, en su pueblo, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultado en Walter Street Burying Ground, Roslindale, Boston, Suffolk, Masachusets, Estados Unidos.
